conbody.p: Overview: Reconciliation Details report enables the CDI Manager to analyze the details around the reconciled encounters and identify gaps in the reconciliation process.

The report provides answers to the following questions.
Which CDSs are not following the reconciliation best practices?
Which CDS could benefit from additional education on the Reconciliation process?
How do we find the gaps in the reconciliation process?
Which cases were incorrectly reconciled?
What is the reimbursement impact the CDS had on a case?
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One Executive Summary Reconciliation Details .

Reconciliation Details displays the following:
KPI Cards: Total Reconciled, #Cases with Financial Impact, Exceptions, and Overall Reimbursement Impact
Encounter Details Tabular Chart
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets generated in the tabular chart.
For filters on all pages, refer .

entry.p: The count of encounters with Reconciled Impact, Reconciled No Impact, or Auto Reconciled status.

row.entry: Number of Cases with Financial Impact = The total count of Reconciled-Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact) encounters with F-DRG WT > R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT – R-DRG WT > 0) and F-DRG WT < R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT – R-DRG WT < 0). Reconciled-Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact) encounters without a shift in the WT (F-DRG WT – R-DRG WT = 0 or F-DRG = R-DRG WT) are not considered in the numerator.

entry.p: The count of incorrectly Reconciled cases.

Exceptions = Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact for Discharge Date < 11/14/2019) cases with no shift in the WT (F-DRG WT = R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT = W-DRG WT when Reconciliation Assessment DRG info is not available)) are highlighted.
Reimbursement Impact = (CMI After CDI - CMI Before CDI) X Blended Rate. E.g. $5,234,786

p.b: Reconciliation Details - Encounter Details Tabular Chart

conbody.p: Info Icon(Text on hover): Encounter Status: Reconciled-Impact, Reconciled-No Impact, Auto-Reconciled

row.entry:
The facility assigned to the encounter in the format Facility Description (Facility ID).
Example: Good Health Hospital (GHH)

The Account Number assigned to the encounter.
Example: ACC2021001

The MRN assigned to the encounter.
Example: MRN2021001

The name of the patient associated with the encounter.
Example: Last Name, First Name

entry.p: The Admission Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
The Discharge Date and Time assigned to the encounter in the facility's timezone.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a discharge date assigned.

row.entry: The length of stay in days for each encounter. LOS value is displayed in blue font, when LOS is greater than GMLOS for the encounter.

entry.p: LOS = Discharge Date/Time – Admit Date/Time
OR
LOS = Last Data Refresh Date/Time – Admit Date/Time
The CDS assigned to the encounter in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa)
The date and time when the initial review was performed on the encounter.
The date is displayed in the Facility's time-zone.

p.note: If the Initial Review Date/time is after the Discharge Date/Time, Initial Review Date/Time is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: The name of the CDS who performed the initial review in the format Last Name, First Name MI (CDS ID).
Example: Ryan, Melissa (rmelissa)
The Tracking Codes assigned to the encounter in comma separated format. You can view a maximum of 10 tracking codes for an encounter.
Example: Quality Review, Prov Education
The rank assigned to the encounter prior to the Initial Review.
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Rank at Initial Review' assigned.
Example: 1, 2, etc.

row.entry:
Displays the number of hours when the encounter status is set as Review Complete Date/Time and when the working review was first created for the selected time period. The date must be in the Facility's time-zone.
Example: 05/24/2020 10:10:00 AM ET.
Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = Review Complete (Date/Time stamp) – Initial Review (Date/Time stamp)

entry.p: The count of Financial Impact clarifications with the status other than DELETED or RETRACTED sent for the encounter.
Example: 2
Note:
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration is a Financial Impact Clarification.
Clarifications with Status 'DELETED' or 'RETRACTED' are excluded from the count.
Clarifications for "Review Not Needed" encounter status are not considered for this metric calculation.

row.entry: Financial Impact Clarifications with response Agreed, Alternative Diagnosis, Other Explanation

entry.p: The count of sent Financial Impact clarifications with Final response as Agreed, Alternative Diagnosis, and Other Explanation.
Example: 2
Note:
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration is a Financial Impact Clarification.
Clarifications with Status 'DELETED' or 'RETRACTED' are excluded from the count.
Clarifications for "Review Not Needed" encounter status are not considered for this metric calculation.
Clarifications that do not have a FINAL response are not considered in this calculation.
The 'primary' Working DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
The WT associated with the 'primary' Working DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393
The 'SOI' from the working review.
Example: 2

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'ROM' from the working review.
Example: 3

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'AMLOS' from the working review.
Example: 3.1

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'GMLOS ' from the working review.
Example: 3.2

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary working DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced working DRG is displayed.

entry.p: The 'primary' Possible DRG along with the description displayed on the Review page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)

p.note: This column displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned if the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: The WT associated with the 'primary' Possible DRG displayed on the Review page.
Example: 1.0393

p.note: This column displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in the orange font for the encounters that have a ‘Working DRG’ assigned if the ‘Possible DRG’ is blank or null.

entry.p: The code and description of the Reconciliation Assessment DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
The WT associated with the Reconciliation Assessment DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393
The 'SOI' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1

p.note: If the SOI associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the SOI associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'ROM' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 1

p.note: If the ROM associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the ROM associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'AMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 3.1

p.note: If the AMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the AMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The 'GMLOS' from the Reconciliation Assessment.
Example: 2.4

p.note: If the GMLOS associated with the primary Reconciliation Assessment DRG is not available, then the GMLOS associated with the advanced Reconciliation Assessment DRG is displayed.

entry.p: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
The code and description of the Final Coded DRG along with the description displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 872 - SEPTICEMIA OR SEVERE SEPSIS WITHOUT MV >96 HOURS WITHOUT MAJOR COMPLICATION OR COMORBIDITY (MCC)
Note:
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'

row.entry:
Displays the type (Medical, Surgical, Unknown) associated with the Final MS-DRG Code.
Values
S - Surgical
M - Medical
U - Unknown

entry.p: The WT associated with the Final Coded DRG displayed on the Reconciliation page.
Example: 1.0393

p.note: This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time

entry.p: The Final Coded 'SOI'.
Example: 2
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced SOI Values are used if Primary SOI values are blank
The Final Coded 'ROM'.
Example: 3
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced ROM Values are used if Primary ROM values are blank
The Final Coded 'AMLOS'.
Example: 3.1
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced AMLOS Values are used if Primary AMLOS values are blank
The Final Coded 'GMLOS'.
Example: 3.2
This column is blank for the encounters that do not have a ‘Initial Review Date/Time'
Advanced GMLOS Values are used if Primary GMLOS values are blank
The shift in the WT at the encounter level.
Example: 1.0178
Encounter Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (or Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank)
Note:
Encounter Variance can be negative, positive, or zero.

p.b: Negative ‘Variance’ is displayed in the red font E.g. - 1.0178.

entry.p: Encounter Variance is not calculated for the encounters that do not have an initial review date.
Review Not Needed Encounters are not considered.
Rounded to 4 decimal places.
The Blended Rate associated with the payer/Grouper assigned to the encounter.
Example: 7512
When the Blended Rate is not available, the Operating Federal rate is displayed.
The ‘Reimbursement Impact’ value for the encounter.
Example: $ 5,234
'Reimbursement Impact' for an encounter is calculated using the below formula:

ul.li: Encounter Reimbursement Impact = Encounter Variance x Blended Rate

li.b: Negative ‘Reimbursement Impact ($)’ is displayed in the red font.

entry.p: The Impact Type assigned to the encounter
The Encounter Status assigned to the encounter.
Example: Reconciled Impact, Auto-Reconciled

row.entry:
The Payer assigned to the encounter in the format Payer Description (Payer ID).
Example: Medicare (MEDC)

The Grouper version associated with the payer assigned to the encounter.
Example: CMS 38.0

The Unit assigned to the encounter.
Example: ICU

The Patient Type assigned to the encounter.
Example: Inpatient

entry.p: The Visit Type assigned to the encounter.
Format: Discharge Status ID- Description
Example: Inpatient (I)

row.entry:
The Display Status assigned to the encounter.
Format: Discharge Status ID- Description
Example: 01- 01 Self Care

ul.li: You can select date from the lookup provided.
Default: Last 3 calendar months date range selected by default.
With the Reset button, you can reset the discharge date range.
Tooltip: Use "Ctrl + Click" to reset the Discharge Date.
Displays both active and inactive facilities in alphabetical order that you have access to.
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Displays both active and inactive payers in alphabetical order associated with the facilities you have access to.
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.

Displays the following values:
Positive Impact
Negative Impact
No Impact

entry.p: By default, all values should be selected.
Positive Impact: Allows you to filter the list of encounters that have a positive reimbursement impact.
Negative Impact: Allows you to filter the list of encounters that have a negative reimbursement impact.
No Impact: Allows you to filter the list of encounters that have $0 reimbursement impact.

Displays the below values for the overall shift in the weight:
Positive Shift
Negative Shift
No Shift
By default, all values should be selected.

li.p: Negative ‘Variance’ is displayed in the red font. E.g. - 1.0178

ul.li: Variance can be negative,positive, or zero

Displays the following values:
Financial Impact
Both Impact
SOI/ROM Impact
No Impact
Unassigned Impact

row.entry:
Displays the list of DRG type (Medical, Surgical, Unknown) associated with the Final DRG Code.
Values:
S
M
U
Default: ALLYou can select single or all DRG Type.

Identifies the incorrectly reconciled Reimbursement Impact cases.
Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both) cases with no shift in the WT (F-DRG WT = R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT = W-DRG WT when Reconciliation Assessment DRG info is not available))
Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Unassigned) cases with no shift in the WT (F-DRG WT = R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT = W-DRG WT when Reconciliation Assessment DRG info is not available)) and no shift in SOI/ROM values.

ul.li: Displays the available patient types. Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Displays the list of available units.
Displays the list of available admit service.
You can search the Admit Service.
Displays the available patient types.Example: ED, IP, etc.
You can select multiple Visit Type.
You can search the Visit Type.

li.p: Displays the available DRG Specialties. Example: Transplant, Surgery, Neurosurgery.

ul.li: You can search the DRG Specialty.

row.entry: The count of encounters with Reconciled Impact, Reconciled No Impact, or Auto Reconciled status.
Reimbursement Impact = (CMI After CDI - CMI Before CDI) X Blended Rate. E.g. $5,234,786

entry.p: P-DRG: Displays the ‘Working DRG’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.
P-DRG WT: Displays the ‘Working DRG WT’ value in orange font for the reconciled encounters with blank ‘Possible DRG’.

row.entry: Displays Initial Review Date/time in red font when the Initial Review Date/Time > Discharge Date/Time.

entry.p: Variance = Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank)
Displays negative variance value in red font.

row.entry: Configured at payer level; When Blended Rate is not available, Operating Federal rate is displayed.

entry.p: Reimbursement Impact = (Final Coded DRG WT - Reconciliation Assessment DRG WT (Working DRG WT when Discharge Date < 11/14/19 or Reconciliation Assessment DRG is blank)) x Blended Rate. E.g. $5321
Displays negative Reimbursement Impact in red font.
Clarification with a standard or custom ‘Primary Type at Reconciliation’ defined as ‘Financial’ in the admin configuration.
Clarification Status: Includes all clarification statuses except 'Removed' or 'Retracted'

row.entry: LOS: Displays Length of Stay in blue font if LOS value greater than F-GMLOS; W-GMLOS value is considered in case of blank F-GMLOS.

The total count of Reconciled-Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact) encounters with positive and negative shifts, that is, ​F-DRG WT > R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT - R-DRG WT > 0) and F-DRG WT < R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT - R-DRG WT < 0)

The count of Reconciled Impact (Impact Type: Financial, Both, Unassigned Impact) cases with no shift in the WT, that is, (F-DRG WT = R-DRG WT (Or F-DRG WT = W-DRG WT when Reconciliation Assessment DRG info is not available)).

Displays the number of hours when the encounter status is set as Review Complete Date/Time and when the working review was first created for the selected time period. The date must be in the Facility's time-zone. Example: 05/24/2020 10:10:00 AM ET.
Number of hours between Review Complete Date/Time and when the Working Review was first created for the selected time period. Review TAT (Hours) for each encounter = ‘Review Complete’ (Date/Time stamp) – Initial Review (Date/Time stamp).
